problem_statement: The document examines how the role of software developers is shifting toward overseeing, auditing, and validating AI/LLM-generated code, and the security and reliability risks that make rigorous human oversight of that code necessary.
magnitude_evidence: GitHub Copilot code evaluated by Pearce et al. found 'up to 40% depending on the prompt—contained security flaws'; also a systematic review found AI-generated code includes security vulnerabilities more frequently than human-written code.
insufficiency_evidence: 'syntactically correct but logically flawed or insecure code' that developers over-trust via the 'false confidence trap', discovering 'subtle security issues later'; models 'replicate patterns seen in their training data without fully understanding the context' propagating poor practices.
solution_mechanisms: AI governance structures and organizational policies
